text:
Iraq: Maj. Gen. Abdi, military governor general of Iraq, has banned the Communist-inspired "Committees for the Defense of the Republic." These committees were formed on an unofficial basis in government departments to weed out anti-Communist "undesirables." Among the prominent committee members have been present Minister of Guidance Samir, and the director general of police.
Army headquarters ordered the arrest of army personnel participating in any demonstrations or meetings.
Iraqi authorities are considering suppression of several Communist-front groups such as the League of Women's Rights, the Federation of Students, and the Democratic Youth Federation.

Yemen- UAR: The Imam has accepted Nasir's invitation to visit him on his return trip from Italy to Yemen, now predicted for early next month.
Nasir probably requested the meeting in order to assure the Imam that UAR support of his son, Crown Prince Badr, is not aimed at usurping the Imam's authority. Reports of UAR connivance with Badr during his father's absence may have disturbed the Imam and influenced his decision to return home by way of Cairo
